title: Only JSON Serializer Exists
description: JsonSerializer exists only and accepts only Pydantic BaseModel — no Protobuf, MsgPack, CBOR, Avro — limits inter-language scenarios
impact: gRPCRequest exists in request_types.py but no codec implements Protobuf; JSON slower and larger than binary alternatives
proposed_solution: Add MsgPackSerializer and ProtobufSerializer options; expand SerializerType enum in config; decouple JsonSerializer from BaseModel to support dict/str
evidence: json_serializer.py:54-57 rejects non-BaseModel in serialize; json_serializer.py:83-86 requires BaseModel subclass in deserialize; request_types.py:49 notes payload converts to Protobuf via future L6 codec

title: SSEStreamCodec Never Tested with Real Streams
description: SSEStreamCodec exists but not tested — encode_stream() decode_stream() _parse_sse_frame() never tested with real chunks. Also no tests for other L6 components (JsonSerializer CompressionAdapter PresentationPipeline presentation_provider)
impact: SSE protocol critical for LLM streaming — parsing errors cause poor user experience; 0% L6 test coverage
proposed_solution: Create tests: (1) encode->decode roundtrip (2) chunked delivery reassembly (3) multiple data: lines (4) non-JSON fallback (5) empty stream (6) serialization roundtrip for each codec (7) pipeline encode/decode/encode_stream/decode_stream
evidence: No test_*.py files test presentation/ anywhere under tests/ or any other path

title: No Adaptive Compression
description: CompressionAdapter supports only gzip/zstd with fixed algorithm at init — does not dynamically select algorithm based on content type or payload size
impact: May use zstd for highly-compressible HTML files (slower than gzip) but it doesn't — 20-50% performance opportunity lost by data type
proposed_solution: Add ContentTypeAnalyzer to read magic bytes or content-type header and select optimal algorithm + level; add Snappy for latency-critical paths
evidence: compression.py:33-39 only two algorithms with fixed values (algorithm + level) that never change dynamically; pipeline.py:70-74 _should_compress checks size only and does not affect algorithm selection

title: No Charset Normalization
description: charset exists in PipelineConfig (utf-8 default) but only used in encode_stream bypass mode (line 142). No automatic encoding conversion when receiving Shift-JIS or Latin-1 data
impact: If client sends Shift-JIS or Latin-1, pipeline does not convert — garbage may reach L5/Session; sse_stream_codec uses errors='replace' causing silent data loss
proposed_solution: Add chardet detection + iconv/conversion between source encoding and utf-8; make config-disablable; link charset to sse_stream_codec decode
evidence: pipeline.py:30 charset=default('utf-8') but never used in normal encode/decode; pipeline.py:142 only used in bypass encode_stream; sse_stream_codec.py:81 errors='replace' hides encoding problems

title: StreamCodec and Serializer Coexistence Ambiguous
description: PresentationPipeline accepts both serializer and stream_codec but unclear which is used in encode_stream/decode_stream — encode_stream logic (lines 148-161) completely bypasses serializer if codec exists. decode_stream (line 183) passes target_type=Any to serializer.deserialize causing crash with JsonSerializer
impact: Wrong linking may cause double-serialization or skip compression entirely; decode_stream crashes with JsonSerializer when using serializer-only without codec due to target_type=Any
proposed_solution: Document clear strategy: (1) if stream_codec exists -> responsible for framing and serializer used inside codec (2) if serializer only -> stream_codec wraps serialize for each item (3) fix target_type=Any in decode_stream to avoid crash with JsonSerializer
evidence: pipeline.py:148-161 encode_stream completely bypasses serializer.serialize(obj) when codec truthy (line 152); pipeline.py:183 decode_stream passes target_type=Any but JsonSerializer.deserialize requires BaseModel subclass

title: No Stream-Level Compression
description: PresentationPipeline.encode_stream does not compress individual frames — self.compressor is never called in the stream encoding path (lines 138-161). However decode_stream (line 177) attempts self.compressor.decompress(frame) on incoming frames. This asymmetry causes stream decode failure
impact: SSE and streaming APIs send uncompressed text — low transfer efficiency; asymmetry: encode_stream never compresses but decode_stream attempts decompression
proposed_solution: Add per-chunk compression in encode_stream with algorithm declared in SSE header or metadata; sync encode with decode
evidence: pipeline.py:138-161 encode_stream never uses self.compressor; pipeline.py:177 decode_stream attempts self.compressor.decompress(frame) but encode_stream never compressed frames at all

title: JsonSerializer BaseModel Constraint Breaks decode_stream
description: JsonSerializer.serialize rejects non-BaseModel and JsonSerializer.deserialize requires target_type as BaseModel subclass — this causes crash in decode_stream (line 183) which passes target_type=Any. Prevents passing plain dict/list/str through pipeline
impact: PresentationPipeline.decode_stream crashes when using serializer-only without codec because target_type=Any fails issubclass check in json_serializer.py:83; Cannot pass plain dict/list/str through pipeline
proposed_solution: Modify JsonSerializer to accept dict via json.dumps/loads while keeping BaseModel support via model_dump_json/model_validate_json; fix pipeline.py:183 to use type() or pass None with fallback
evidence: json_serializer.py:54-57 if not isinstance(obj, BaseModel): raise; json_serializer.py:83-86 if not issubclass(target_type, BaseModel): raise; pipeline.py:183 yield self.serializer.deserialize(data, target_type=Any)
column17: CompressionAdapter.decompress always uses self.algorithm and ignores actual data magic bytes — if payload compressed with gzip but CompressionAdapter configured for ZSTD, decompression fails or produces corrupted data
column20: Silent data corruption or decompression failure; pipeline.decode (lines 125-127) checks magic bytes to know 'if' data compressed but does not select appropriate algorithm — relies on self.compressor.algorithm only
column23: Add magic-byte detection in CompressionAdapter.decompress by checking first 2-4 bytes for gzip (1f8b) or zstd (28b52ffd) and selecting correct method automatically; remove dependency on self.algorithm for decompress
column24: compression.py:54-67 decompress uses self.algorithm directly without magic bytes check; pipeline.py:43-44 _MAGIC_GZIP and _MAGIC_ZSTD defined but not used to select algorithm in decompression

title: Zero Unit Tests for Entire L6 Layer
description: No unit or integration tests for JsonSerializer SSEStreamCodec CompressionAdapter PresentationPipeline presentation_provider nor any test file covering presentation/
impact: 0% test coverage for any codec or pipeline — simple changes may break serialization or streaming undetected; PRS-002 covers SSE only but issue is broader
proposed_solution: Create comprehensive suite: (1) JsonSerializer roundtrip with BaseModel/dict (2) SSEStreamCodec encode->decode with real chunks and delay (3) CompressionAdapter gzip+zstd roundtrip + auto-detect (4) PresentationPipeline encode/decode/encode_stream/decode_stream with all combinations (5) presentation_provider for each direction (6) bypass mode tests (7) edge cases: empty/None/malformed/large data
evidence: glob search for test*.py under src/ returns nothing for presentation; tests/ contains only test_live_protocol_switch.py which does not test L6 directly; .docs/usage_integration_examples/tests/ does not test any codec directly

title: SSEStreamCodec Drops Event/ID/Retry Fields
description: SSEStreamCodec processes only data: fields and ignores event: id: retry: — encode_stream does not emit them decode_stream does not parse them — breaks EventSource API compatibility
impact: Incompatible with browsers and SSE clients; cannot route different events (chat.completion vs error) via SSE event types
proposed_solution: Add support for event:/id:/retry: in encode_stream (parameters event_type/event_id) and decode_stream (_parse_sse_frame collects all fields in unified dict with data key) maintaining backward compatibility
evidence: sse_stream_codec.py:49 emits only 'data: ...\\n\\n' without event:/id:/retry:; sse_stream_codec.py:86-93 _parse_sse_frame collects only lines.startswith('data:') and ignores other SSE boundaries

title: Bypass Mode encode_stream Crashes on Non-Serializable Objects
description: In bypass mode encode_stream (line 142) attempts chunk.encode(charset) for any non-bytes non-str — crashes if chunk is dict/list/int/None
impact: If INPROCESS direction used with encode_stream on non-bytes stream (e.g. dict chunks) -> AttributeError: 'dict' object has no attribute 'encode' — incomplete bypass implementation
dependencies: pipeline.py:141-143 yield chunk if isinstance(chunk, bytes) else chunk.encode(self.config.charset) assumes non-bytes are always str
proposed_solution: Add isinstance(chunk, str) guard before .encode() call. For non-str/non-bytes types, use json.dumps() or str() as fallback. Raise clear PresentationError only if all conversions fail
column15: encode_stream Wraps Each Item in Individual Async Generator
column16: encode_stream wraps each object in _single_item_stream and passes to codec.encode_stream creating N separate async generators (one per item) instead of passing original stream directly
column19: Overhead of creating and destroying async generator per item reduces efficiency for long streams (millions of items); prevents codec from seeing full stream for aggregation or caching
column20: pipeline.py:156 codec.encode_stream(self._single_item_stream(raw)) for each item; pipeline.py:189-192 _single_item_stream yields single item then closes
column22: Remove _single_item_stream wrapper and pass obj_stream directly to codec.encode_stream after optional serialization; modify encode_stream signature to support pre-processing for each element inside codec itself if needed

title: presentation_provider bypass_inprocess Parameter is Dead Config
description: The bypass_inprocess parameter in build_presentation_pipeline does not affect PresentationPipeline behavior — pipeline decides bypass itself based on Direction.INPROCESS in _is_bypass(). The conditional branch (lines 75-77) only does debug log
impact: Developer confusion: parameter exists in public API but does nothing; users think disabling it prevents bypass but pipeline bypasses anyway
dependencies: presentation_provider.py:75-77 if bypass_inprocess and direction == INPROCESS does nothing; pipeline.py:64-68 _is_bypass() decides bypass autonomously without external config
proposed_solution: Remove bypass_inprocess parameter from build_presentation_pipeline entirely or wire it into PipelineConfig as real flag that _is_bypass() checks centralizing all bypass decisions in pipeline itself

title: No Metrics or Monitoring Hooks in Pipeline
description: PresentationPipeline contains no callbacks/events/metrics hooks to measure compression ratio serialization latency throughput or error rates - cannot monitor L6 performance in production
impact: No production visibility on L6 performance — cannot detect serialization bottlenecks or silent compression failures; relies only on logging.debug
dependencies: pipeline contains no metrics/counter/timer; no Prometheus/OpenTelemetry integration anywhere in pipeline or codecs
proposed_solution: Add optional Counter/Timer hooks in pipeline.encode/decode/encode_stream/decode_stream measuring: (1) pre/post compression size (2) serialization duration (3) errors per codec (4) bytes processed; use structlog or OpenTelemetry spans with optional injection via PipelineConfig

title: SSEStreamCodec encode_stream Crashes on Non-Serializable Objects
description: SSEStreamCodec.encode_stream calls json.dumps(obj) directly at line 45 on non-bytes non-str objects when auto_json=True — raises TypeError if obj contains non-serializable types (Callable set datetime complex)
impact: Streaming crashes on non-JSON-serializable objects; InProcessRequest.target excluded (exclude=True) but arbitrary objects outside control
dependencies: sse_stream_codec.py:44-45 if self.auto_json: data_str = json.dumps(obj, ensure_ascii=False); no str(obj)/repr(obj) fallback
proposed_solution: Wrap json.dumps in try/except TypeError with str(obj)/repr(obj) fallback; log serialization failure at warning level; auto_json=False prevents this error

title: decode_stream Passes Decompressed Data Back to Codec Without Re-Compression
description: decode_stream (line 177) decompresses frame then passes decompressed data to codec.decode_stream — but encode_stream never compressed frames (PRS-006). This asymmetry causes stream decode failure
impact: If stream compressed at message level (encode/decode) and passed via streaming, decode_stream attempts decompression of frame that may not be compressed — may produce corrupted data or raise decompress error
dependencies: pipeline.py:177 data = self.compressor.decompress(frame) if self.compressor and self._is_compressed(frame) else frame — _is_compressed guard prevents damage but relies on magic bytes that may coincidentally match start of uncompressed data
proposed_solution: Add explicit X-Compression header in first SSE frame (e.g. X-Compression: gzip/zstd/none) so decode_stream knows whether stream is compressed; fully synchronize encode_stream and decode_stream on compression behavior (both compress or neither)

title: CompressionAdapter Lacks Edge Case Handling for Empty/Truncated Data
description: CompressionAdapter.compress/decompress does not handle: (1) empty data b'' (2) truncated data (3) corrupted data with correct magic bytes but invalid content — gzip/zstd raise uncaught exceptions
impact: Empty data produces non-zero compressed size (gzip produces 20 bytes for empty input) causing bloat; truncated data causes OSError/gzip.Error/zstd.Error uncaught by PresentationError
dependencies: compression.py:41-52 compress does not handle b'' as edge case; compression.py:54-67 decompress does not handle truncated/corrupted data except generic try/except
proposed_solution: Add is_empty = len(data) == 0 -> return b'' directly in compress/decompress without algorithm; add explicit catch for gzip.BadGzipFile and zstandard.ZstdError before generic except
